rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication. Granted. As to the first and fourth causes of action, summary adjudication is granted. Defendant has produced prima facie evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish the element of damages (i.e. that Plaintiffs could have achieved a better result in the underlying Killips litigation but for Defendant's negligence). (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 26; see also Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe v. Superior Court (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1052, 1057.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs do not dispute that the Killips litigation was settled, and Plaintiffs were dismissed with prejudice without having to pay any money to settle or incur liability. (UMF 26.) Further, the affidavits of Ms. Meier and Ms. Selig-Farney do not show that Plaintiffs have paid attorneys' fees to their current counsel as result of Defendant's alleged negligence. (See Declaration of Selig-Farney, 22-27; Declaration of Meier, 22-30; see also Orrick, supra, 107 Cal.App.4th at 1060.) As to the second and third causes of action, summary adjudication is granted. Plaintiffs have failed to produce sufficient evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact exists regarding monetary damages for Defendant's alleged misrepresentation. (Id.) As to the fifth cause of action, summary adjudication is granted. Based on the evidence, severe emotional distress cannot be found as a matter of law. (Fletcher v. Western National Life Insurance Company (1970) 10 Cal.App.3d 376, 397.) The pleadings set the boundaries of the issues to be resolved at summary judgment. (Conroy v. Regents of University of Cal. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 1244, 1254.) Plaintiffs allege in their Complaint that their severe emotional distress was caused by the loss of their company. (Complaint 86.) However, Plaintiffs' affidavits in support of their Opposition state they suffered severe emotional distress over the potential loss of their company, which contradict the allegations in their Complaint. (See Declaration of Meier 31; Declaration of Selig-Farney 28.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads compliance with the Government Claims Act claims presentation requirements. Whether the senior clerk typist was authorized to accept service on behalf of Defendant pursuant to Govt. Code sec. 915(a) is a factual issue that goes beyond the scope of demurrer. The SAC pleads that Govt. Code sec. 915(a) was complied with. (SAC, 5; Declaration of John C. Ellis (filed as an attachment to the SAC), 4.) As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC adequately pleads facts establishing the elements of Govt. Code sec. 815.2. The SAC pleads that Defendant and its employees had a legal duty to surrounding property owners (SAC, 25), that an employee or agent of Defendant breached that duty by generating sparks while doing renovation on the building (SAC, 22), and that the sparks started the fire that caused the property damage. (SAC, 22.) As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC adequately pleads facts establishing the elements of Govt. Code sec. 815.4. The SAC pleads that Defendant and its agents had a duty to maintain a safe working environment at the construction site, as well as to the adjacent properties (SAC, 36), Bollo Construction, an independent contractor of Defendant, breached its duty by generating sparks (SAC, 32), and the sparks caused the ignition of the fire. (SAC, 32.) As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The SAC does not allege a statutory basis for its subrogation claim against a government entity. (See Hoff v. Vacaville Unified School Dist. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 925, 932; "In the absence of a constitutional requirement, public entities may be held liable only if a statute ... is found declaring them to be liable.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. 1015 PIERCE STREET INVESTORS LP et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT ROBERT OVEREND's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Both the notice of demurrer and memorandum of points and authorities in support of demurrer state that Defendant is demurring to the First Amended Complaint. A Second Amended Complaint was filed on October 16, 2013. Additionally, Defendant does not cite to an existing statute as the basis for his demurrer in violation of CCP sec. 430.60. The notice of the demurrer states the demurrer is being brought per "CCP 430(E)." rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT LEM WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH DICKSON VS. BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Pursuant to CCP 452, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action of disability discrimination. Those facts also support the other causes of action. Defendant raises factual issues and many of the facts that Defendant states are missing are presumptively within its knowledge. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CYPRESS GG2'S Motion To Vacate Appraisal Award. Denied. See Motion, Exhibits 1, 4; Webb Decl., Exhibit 1.)
